perturbation.original_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client.
perturbation.changed_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client.
perturbation.explanation: By removing the phrase "unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client,", Chemsultants is now absolutely obligated to keep all confidential information secret, even if the Client provides written permission to disclose. This in-text contradiction makes it impossible to know under which circumstances Chemsultants can disclose confidential information. The original phrase allowed flexibility, and now that flexibility is revoked and created a contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 2. Use of Confidential Information. The parties acknowledge that any use of shared Confidential Information other than for the benefit of Client or the furtherance of a business relationship with Client, would be wrongful and cause irreparable harm to Client. Accordingly, Chemsultants agrees to (a) keep all Confidential Information confidential and to not, at any time during or after any transaction where Confidential Information disclosures are made, publish, disclose, or divulge Confidential Information to any person, firm, or corporation other than its Representatives and to cause its Representatives to observe the terms of this Agreement; (b) not use, directly or indirectly, copy, or reproduce any Confidential Information of Client for its own benefit or for the benefit of any person, firm, or corporation other than Client, and in any event only in connection with evaluating or engaging in a transaction or business relationship with Client. Chemsultants hereby agrees it shall be responsible for any breach by it or its Representatives. For the purposes of this Agreement, the term “Representative” shall mean the party’s affiliates, directors, officers, employees, agents, advisors (including, without limitation, financial advisors, legal counsel and accountants) and controlling persons, who are actively and directly participating in any transaction with Client or who otherwise need to know the Confidential Information for the purpose of furthering any transaction with Client, and who agree to be bound by the terms of this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text states that Chemsultants agrees to keep confidential information confidential 'unless otherwise agreed to in writing by Client'. The changed text removes this exception, creating an absolute obligation. This directly contradicts the possibility, implied in both the original and contradicted text, that the Client could provide written permission to disclose confidential information. The change introduces a clear and direct conflict, making it impossible to determine under what circumstances, if any, Chemsultants can disclose confidential information, creating a strong in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 5. Return of Documents. Upon written request by Client at any time, Chemsultants shall promptly return all Confidential Information, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in the party’s p o s s e s s i o n o r i n t h e p o s s e s s i o n o f a n y R e p r e s e n t a t i v e o f t h e p a r t y , p r o v i d e d , however, Chemsultants shall be permitted to charge Client for all reasonable costs incurred by Chemsultants for compliance with this Section.
perturbation.changed_text: 5. Return of Documents. Upon written request by Client at any time, Chemsultants may, at its sole discretion, return all Confidential Information, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in the party’s p o s s e s s i o n o r i n t h e p o s s e s s i o n o f a n y R e p r e s e n t a t i v e o f t h e p a r t y , p r o v i d e d , however, Chemsultants shall be permitted to charge Client for all reasonable costs incurred by Chemsultants for compliance with this Section.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "shall promptly return" to "may, at its sole discretion, return" makes the return of confidential information optional. However, Chemsultants is still permitted to charge Client for the costs of compliance. This creates an in-text contradiction, because why should the client be charged for costs associated with document return, if Chemsultants has discretion as to weather or not these documents are being returned? This creates a contradictory and questionable burden and right to charge for the costs.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 5. Return of Documents. Upon written request by Client at any time, Chemsultants may, at its sole discretion, return all Confidential Information, including all copies, reproductions, summaries, analyses or extracts thereof or based thereon in the party’s p o s s e s s i o n o r i n t h e p o s s e s s i o n o f a n y R e p r e s e n t a t i v e o f t h e p a r t y , p r o v i d e d , however, Chemsultants shall be permitted to charge Client for all reasonable costs incurred by Chemsultants for compliance with this Section.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original obligation of Chemsultants to 'shall promptly return' confidential information to 'may, at its sole discretion, return'. This creates a strong contradiction because the original text obligates Chemsultants to return the documents. However, Chemsultants retains the right to charge the client for compliance with this section, which appears in both the Changed Text and Contradicted Text, even though it has the sole discretion to return (or not) the documents. The justification explains that charging for compliance with a discretionary action creates a questionable burden and right to charge, and is an in-text contradiction according to the given definition of Structural Flaws.

perturbation.original_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and cannot be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part unless approved in writing. This Agreement may not be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, without consent, provided such purchaser agrees to be bound by all terms and conditions contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and can be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part orally or written . This Agreement may be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, with the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall not be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, with consent, provided such purchaser agrees to be bound by all terms and conditions contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: By changing "cannot be modified unless approved in writing" to "can be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part orally or written" directly contradicts the initial sentence stating that the agreement cannot be modified unless in writing. Similarly, the phrase "may not be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party" to "may be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, with the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall not be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, with consent". This contradiction means the reader is unable to know if the agreement can be transfered.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 9. Modification. This Agreement contains the entire agreement between the parties concerning the confidentiality of the Confidential Information and can be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part orally or written . This Agreement may be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, with the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall not be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, with consent, provided such purchaser agrees to be bound by all terms and conditions contained herein.
perturbation.justification: The changed text introduces a clear contradiction. The original text states the agreement 'cannot be modified unless approved in writing', while the changed text says it 'can be modified, released, discharged, abandoned, or otherwise terminated in whole or in part orally or written'. This directly contradicts the requirement for written modification. Furthermore, the original text states the agreement 'may not be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, without the prior written consent of the other party', while the changed text indicates that the agreement 'may be assigned by either party, by operation of law or otherwise, with the prior written consent of the other party, and any attempt to do so shall be void, provided, however, that either party shall not be permitted to assign this agreement to any purchaser of all or substantially all of such party’s assets or equity interests, with consent'. This change in permissions regarding assignment creates a direct contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: 11. Term. This Agreement shall become effective upon execution by the parties and shall continue in full force and effect for a period of two (2) years from the date thereof, provided however, that the obligations of the parties contained herein shall continue for a period of five (5) years following the date of such termination.
perturbation.changed_text: 11. Term. This Agreement shall become effective upon execution by the parties and shall continue in full force and effect for a period of two (2) years from the date thereof, provided however, that the obligations of the parties contained herein shall terminate immediately following the date of such termination.
perturbation.explanation: Changing "the obligations of the parties contained herein shall continue for a period of five (5) years following the date of such termination" to "the obligations of the parties contained herein shall terminate immediately following the date of such termination" creates an in-text contradiction. This directly creates an immediate termination instead of the original five years.
perturbation.contradicted_text: 11. Term. This Agreement shall become effective upon execution by the parties and shall continue in full force and effect for a period of two (2) years from the date thereof, provided however, that the obligations of the parties contained herein shall terminate immediately following the date of such termination.
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies section 11 to state that obligations terminate immediately following the agreement's termination. The contradicted text (which is the same as the changed text in this case, but that doesn't mean there wasn't a contradiction) also states that obligations terminate immediately, which directly contradicts the original text which stated the obligations continue for five years following the termination of the agreement as explained in the Perturbation Explanation, which clearly created a contradiction.
